1	# Artifact Generalization & Three-Layer Run Architecture
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-29
4	Status: Approved (refined through 3 review rounds with codex + fresh-eyes)
5	
6	## Summary
7	
8	Two threads converge in this plan:
9	
10	1. **Generalize artifacts beyond markdown documents.** Diagrams, wireframes, hero images, prompts — text and binary — become first-class artifacts via media-type plurality and sidecar `.ddx.yaml` files. Generated artifacts are checked into git; regeneration is source-hash-driven.
11	2. **Three-layer run architecture.** DDx owns three explicit layered primitives: `ddx run` (single agent invocation), `ddx try <bead>` (bead attempt in isolated worktree), `ddx work` (queue drain). The current two-class storage split (`.ddx/exec-runs/` vs `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/`) collapses into one substrate.
12	
13	These threads converge: **generating an artifact is a layer-1 agent run** with `produces_artifact` metadata.
14	
15	## Decisions (resolved through review)
16	
17	- **Artifact authority:** identity present → artifact. No "managed asset" second-class concept.
18	- **FEAT-019 fate:** child of FEAT-010 (not peer). Contracts to evaluation UX; workflow shapes (comparison, replay, benchmark) move to skills library.
19	- **Server posture:** 100% read coverage on HTTP/MCP for all DDx state, sequenced by impact. Writes case-by-case as workflows demand. Only `artifactRegenerate` write surface added in this plan.
20	- **Generated artifacts:** committed to git unconditionally. Size guard (50MB warn / 100MB hard error) with actionable git-lfs guidance; no auto-init / auto-`.gitattributes`.
21	- **CONTRACT-003:** amendments allowed if FEAT-006 work surfaces gaps. The mountable Cobra root export (below) is a load-bearing audit item.
22	- **Run substrate:** single on-disk shape; layer-2 and layer-3 add metadata. `.ddx/exec-runs/` and `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` collapse.
23	- **No run-type catalog beyond the three layers.** Comparison, replay, benchmark, etc. are skill compositions, never enshrined in Go core or specs.
24	- **Loop ownership sharpened:** DDx owns mechanical queue drain (`ddx work`); content-aware supervisory decisions (e.g., "comparison failed → enqueue reconciliation beads") remain plugin/HELIX territory.
25	- **CLI verbs:** `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work` promoted to top-level. Hard-deprecate `ddx agent run`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx agent execute-loop` (exit non-zero with bare redirect message — no aliases, no grace period).
26	- **Generate-artifact layer placement:** pure-return generators (agent returns bytes/text, DDx writes file) are layer-1. Repo-editing generators are layer-2 with bead-less worktree.
27	- **Quorum dispatch:** moves to skill composition. `--quorum` flag goes away; replaced by `compare-prompts` skill.
28	- **`ddx agent` passthrough is structural:** DDx imports the upstream agent module's Cobra root and mounts it under `ddx agent`. Notionally `cli(ddx).agent(load_cli(agent))`. DDx does not enumerate per-subcommand wrappers; the entire upstream tree is the source of truth.
29	
30	## Three-layer architecture
31	
32	| Layer | CLI | Inputs | Outputs / side effects | Owns |
33	|---|---|---|---|---|
34	| 1 | `ddx run` | prompt + agent config | structured output, side effects, run metadata (tokens, model, duration, exit) | invocation atom; consumes upstream `ddx-agent` per CONTRACT-003 |
35	| 2 | `ddx try <bead>` | bead id, base revision | new worktree state + agent-run evidence; merge or preserve | worktree start/end capture; bead → prompt resolution; side-effect bundling |
36	| 3 | `ddx work` | bead queue, stop conditions | sequence of `ddx try` records + loop-level record (drained / blocked / deferred) | mechanical queue drain; no-progress detection |
37	
38	`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`. `ddx work` iterates `ddx try`. One on-disk substrate; layer metadata distinguishes records.
39	
40	`ddx artifact regenerate <id>` is sugar over layer 1 (or layer 2 if the generator edits the repo) with `produces_artifact: <id>` metadata.
41	
42	## Frame artifact updates
43	
44	### docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md
45	- Thesis: "documents AI agents consume" → "artifacts agents produce and consume."
46	- Artifact-management bullet: non-document media + generators.
47	- One sentence on producer/consumer space (no separate matrix).
48	- New Design-Philosophy subsection: **Three-layer run architecture** — `run` / `try` / `work` named as DDx's loop ownership.
49	- KVPs: "Multi-media artifact graph"; "Three-layer run architecture (run/try/work)"; note invocation is upstream.
50	- Principle #1: "Artifacts are the product (documents primary)."
51	
52	### docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md
53	- Summary: artifacts (multi-media); three-layer run architecture; one substrate.
54	- Problem: add "no provenance for generated artifacts."
55	- Goals: reword #1; add three-layer-architecture goal; add generated-artifact-regeneration goal; add "100% read coverage on HTTP/MCP."
56	- Non-goals: sharpen loop wording (mechanical drain in scope; supervisory content-aware decisions out). Add: "DDx does not catalog run types beyond the three layers."
57	- FEAT-001/005/006/007/008/010/019/021 blurbs updated.
58	
59	### FEAT-001-cli.md
60	- Promote `run` / `try` / `work` to top-level.
61	- `ddx agent` mounts upstream Cobra root structurally; no DDx-defined leaf subcommands beneath it.
62	- Hard-deprecation handlers for `ddx agent {run, execute-bead, execute-loop}`.
63	- New namespaces: `ddx runs` (cross-layer evidence introspection), `ddx tries` (layer-2 specifically), `ddx work workers` (layer-3 worker management).
64	- Remove FEAT-001:92 backward-compatibility clause for `ddx agent execute-loop`.
65	
66	### FEAT-005-artifacts.md
67	- Identity broadens to non-markdown via sidecar `.ddx.yaml`.
68	- Add `media_type` and `generated_by` fields.
69	- Authority rule documented: identity present → artifact.
70	- Terminology cleanup ("identity," not "ddx: identity").
71	
72	### FEAT-006-agent-service.md
73	- Tighten to layer-1 consumer-side wrapper that powers `ddx run`.
74	- `ddx agent` is structural passthrough (mount upstream Cobra root).
75	- Non-bead Profile/Permissions selection (artifact-keyed path).
76	- Migration table: what stays in FEAT-006 (CONTRACT-003 boundary, profile/permissions), what moves to FEAT-010 (worktree, merge/preserve, evidence bundles, queue drain), what moves to FEAT-001 (CLI surface).
77	- Session-log boundary clarified: DDx owns the envelope/pointer in evidence bundles; upstream owns inner log shape.
78	
79	### FEAT-010-executions.md (substantial refactor)
80	- Three-layer architecture explicit and load-bearing.
81	- Substrate unification: one record shape; layer metadata.
82	- Migrate `.ddx/exec-runs/` and `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` to one layout.
83	- Specify `ddx work` no-progress / stop conditions.
84	- Narrow read-only amendment for `artifactRegenerate` only.
85	- No run-type catalog beyond the three layers.
86	
87	### FEAT-007-doc-graph.md
88	- Sidecar scanner refactor; `media_type`; `generated_by` edge with separate staleness rule (provenance, not authority — does not cascade like `depends_on`); version bump on Complete-status feature.
89	- 100% read endpoints for graph state, generated-artifact metadata, sidecar content.
90	
91	### FEAT-008-web-ui.md
92	- Media-type-aware rendering (markdown, mermaid SVG, excalidraw embed, image inline, PDF embed).
93	- Regenerate action wired to `artifactRegenerate` mutation; reconcile with existing FEAT-008:391 weak Re-run requirement.
94	- Layer-aware run views (`work` → `try` → `run` drill-down).
95	
96	### FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md
97	- Contract to evaluation UX (comparison views, grading rubric storage and display, benchmark result aggregation).
98	- Workflow shapes → skills library.
99	- Remove peer-to-FEAT-010 assertions (FEAT-019:40, :404).
100	
101	### FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md
102	- Layer-aware run-history routes against unified substrate.
103	
104	### FEAT-014, FEAT-013
105	- One-sentence touches: cost attribution and concurrency for generate-artifact.
106	
107	## `ddx agent` subcommand fates
108	
109	`ddx agent` becomes a structural mount of the upstream agent CLI Cobra root. Per-file fate in DDx:
110	
111	**Delete with prejudice (upstream owns these via mounted subtree):**
112	- `agent_list.go`
113	- `agent_capabilities.go`
114	- `agent_doctor.go`
115	- `agent_check.go`
116	- `agent_models.go`
117	- `agent_providers.go`
118	- `agent_route_status.go`
119	- `agent_catalog.go`
120	- `agent_reindex.go`
121	- `agent_benchmark.go` (replaced by `benchmark-suite` skill)
122	- `agent_replay.go` (replaced by `replay-bead` skill)
123	
124	**Move to other DDx namespaces (DDx-only concerns):**
125	- `agent_executions.go` → `tries_*.go`
126	- `agent_log.go` → `runs_log.go`
127	- `agent_metrics.go` → `runs_metrics.go`
128	- `agent_usage.go` → folded into `runs_metrics.go`
129	- `agent_workers.go` → `work_workers.go`
130	
131	**DDx-side overrides on the mounted subtree:**
132	- Deprecation handlers for `agent run` / `agent execute-bead` / `agent execute-loop`.
133	- `agent condense` (DDx-specific output filter — keep as override or move upstream during impl pass).
134	
135	**`agent_cmd.go` itself thins to:** mount upstream Cobra root + register DDx overrides.
136	
137	## Skill library (DDx-shipped workflows)
138	
139	Per FEAT-011's path model: source at `skills/ddx/`, embedded at `cli/internal/skills/ddx/`, init-time copies to `.claude/skills/ddx/`, `.agents/skills/ddx/`, `.ddx/skills/ddx/`.
140	
141	- `compare-prompts` — N-arm dispatch + aggregation (replaces `--quorum` flag and `agent benchmark`)
142	- `replay-bead` — re-run with altered conditions, baseline diff (replaces `agent replay`)
143	- `benchmark-suite` — compare across prompt matrix
144	- `adversarial-review`
145	- `effort-estimate`
146	- `bead-breakdown`
147	
148	## Bead breakdown
149	
150	**Frame updates (sequential where dependencies require):**
151	
152	1. Update product-vision.md (+ three-layer subsection)
153	2. Update prd.md (+ three-layer goal, sharpened loop non-goal, no-catalog non-goal)
154	3. Update FEAT-001 (CLI surface refactor; remove backcompat clause; new namespaces) — **hard predecessor for #6, #7**
155	4. Update FEAT-005 (multi-media identity refactor)
156	5. CONTRACT-003 audit — confirm `ddx-agent` exports a mountable Cobra root and other gaps
157	6. *(Conditional)* Open CONTRACT-003 amendment in `~/Projects/agent` if #5 surfaces gaps
158	7. Update FEAT-006 (layer-1 consumer; structural passthrough; non-bead Profile selection; migration table; session-log envelope boundary)
159	8. Refactor FEAT-010 (three-layer architecture; substrate unification; on-disk migration; no-progress stop conditions; narrow `artifactRegenerate` amendment)
160	9. Update FEAT-007 (sidecar refactor; media_type; generated_by; separate staleness; version bump)
161	10. Contract FEAT-019 to evaluation UX
162	11. Update FEAT-008 (media rendering, regenerate action, layer-aware run views)
163	12. Update FEAT-021 (layer-aware run-history routes)
164	13. Update FEAT-014 (cost attribution for generate-artifact)
165	14. Update FEAT-013 (concurrency note for regenerate)
166	15. **Repo-wide audit & verb migration** — atomic with #3:
167	    - Delete DDx reimplementations of upstream subcommands (list above).
168	    - Migrate DDx-only run/evidence subcommands to `runs`/`tries`/`work workers`.
169	    - Register DDx overrides on the mounted subtree (deprecations + condense).
170	    - Delete `agent_reindex.go` outright.
171	    - Update scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to new verbs.
172	
173	**Design artifacts (concurrent with frame):**
174	
175	16. Architecture: artifact identity schema (concurrent with #4)
176	17. Architecture: graph schema with media_type + generated_by + separate staleness (concurrent with #9)
177	18. Architecture: three-layer run substrate — record shape, layer metadata, on-disk layout, migration, `ddx work` stop conditions (concurrent with #8)
178	19. Sidecar `.ddx.yaml` schema design
179	
180	**Read-coverage thread:**
181	
182	20. Read-coverage audit — enumerate every CLI-visible read; map to HTTP/MCP coverage; identify gaps
183	21. Per-gap beads — sequenced by user-visibility impact, land case-by-case (this is a pointer to ongoing work, not a single bead)
184	
185	**Skill library:**
186	
187	22. `compare-prompts` skill
188	23. `replay-bead` skill
189	24. `benchmark-suite` skill
190	25. `adversarial-review` skill
191	26. `effort-estimate` skill
192	27. `bead-breakdown` skill
193	
194	**Implementation (P2, after frame & design):**
195	
196	- CLI: `ddx run` / `ddx try` top-level commands; mount upstream agent Cobra root; deprecation handlers
197	- CLI: `ddx runs` / `ddx tries` / `ddx work workers` namespaces (subsuming migrated subcommands)
198	- CLI: `ddx artifact regenerate <id>`, `ddx artifact list --media-type <t>`
199	- Storage: sidecar reader/writer; size-guard threshold/warning/tests
200	- Graph: `generated_by` edges + provenance staleness rule
201	- Run framework: refactor to one substrate per #18; generator metadata; `ddx work` no-progress detection
202	- Server: `artifactRegenerate` write endpoint
203	- Server UI: media-type renderers; regenerate action; layer-aware run history
204	- Migration: passive `media_type` only (read-time inference from extension); on-disk run-record migration per #18
205	
206	## Constraints
207	
208	- Beads #3, #7, #8, #15 must merge atomically — old verbs are referenced in scripts/library/personas/init help text/CI; an isolated #3 break the in-flight `ddx-refine-plan` skill that produced this plan.
209	- Bead #5 (CONTRACT-003 audit) gates #7 (FEAT-006). #6 conditional.
210	- Architecture sketches (#16–#19) run concurrent with their corresponding frame beads, not after — keeps frame/design from drifting.
211	- Read-coverage epic (#20–#21) is independent and not gated on this plan.
212	
213	## Refinement provenance
214	
215	Refined through 3 rounds of adversarial review (codex via `ddx agent run --harness codex` + fresh-eyes general-purpose subagent in parallel) plus user-resolved questions Q1–Q12. Key user decisions: artifact authority (Q1), FEAT-019 child-of-010 (Q2), reads-matter-writes-case-by-case (Q3), check-in unconditionally (Q4), CONTRACT-003 amendments allowed (Q5), substrate unification (Q6), no run-type catalog (Q7), no v1-gate framing (Q8), FEAT-019 contracts (Q9), generate-artifact split layer-1/layer-2 (Q10), quorum-as-skill (Q11), structural passthrough not per-command (Q12 follow-up).

input.prompt:
I need a comprehensive enumeration of three things in this Go CLI repo at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1:

1. **All CLI-visible read surfaces**: List every `ddx` subcommand that reads/queries state (not write/mutation commands). Look in `cli/cmd/` for all .go files. For each command file, note the command name and what data it reads. Focus on list/show/get commands, not create/update/delete.

2. **All HTTP routes**: Look in `cli/internal/server/` for route definitions. List every GET route with its path and what data it serves.

3. **All MCP tools**: Look for MCP tool definitions anywhere in the codebase. List every tool name and what data it reads.

Please search thoroughly - check cli/cmd/*.go, cli/internal/server/**/*.go, cli/internal/mcp/**/*.go, and any GraphQL schema files. Report file paths and line numbers for key findings.

90	// New creates a new DDx server bound to addr, serving data from workingDir.
91	func New(addr, workingDir string) *Server {
92		nodeName := resolveNodeName()
93		stateDir := serverAddrDir() // XDG-standard user-level dir, one per node
94		state := loadServerState(stateDir, nodeName)
95		state.workingDir = workingDir
96	
97		workers := NewWorkerManager(workingDir)
98		workers.ReconcileStaleWorkers()
99		beadHub := bead.NewWatcherHub(250 * time.Millisecond)
100		s := &Server{
101			Addr:       addr,
102			WorkingDir: workingDir,
103			mux:        http.NewServeMux(),
104			startTime:  time.Now().UTC(),
105			workers:    workers,
106			beadHub:    beadHub,
107			state:      state,
108		}
109		state.coordinatorReg = workers.LandCoordinators
110	
111		// Register the server's own project immediately.
112		state.RegisterProject(workingDir)
113		_ = state.save()
114	
115		s.routes()
116		return s
117	}

3034	func (s *Server) mcpCallTool(params json.RawMessage, r *http.Request) mcpToolResult {
3035		var call struct {
3036			Name      string         `json:"name"`
3037			Arguments map[string]any `json:"arguments"`
3038		}
3039		if err := json.Unmarshal(params, &call); err != nil {
3040			return mcpToolResult{
3041				Content: []mcpContent{mcpText("invalid tool call parameters")},
3042				IsError: true,
3043			}
3044		}
3045	
3046		// Tools that operate on project-local data resolve the optional "project"
3047		// argument first; tools listed in the default branch are host/user global
3048		// and ignore project scoping entirely.
3049		project, _ := call.Arguments["project"].(string)
3050	
3051		switch call.Name {
3052		case "ddx_list_projects":
3053			return s.mcpListProjects()
3054		case "ddx_show_project":
3055			id, _ := call.Arguments["id"].(string)
3056			path, _ := call.Arguments["path"].(string)
3057			return s.mcpShowProject(id, path)
3058		case "ddx_provider_list":
3059			return s.mcpProviderList()
3060		case "ddx_provider_show":
3061			harness, _ := call.Arguments["harness"].(string)
3062			return s.mcpProviderShow(harness)
3063		}
3064	
3065		// From here on: project-local tools. Resolve the project arg to a working
3066		// directory before dispatching.
3067		workingDir, errResult := s.mcpResolveWorkingDir(project)
3068		if errResult != nil {
3069			return *errResult
3070		}
3071	
3072		switch call.Name {
3073		case "ddx_list_documents":
3074			return s.mcpListDocuments(workingDir)
3075		case "ddx_read_document":
3076			path, _ := call.Arguments["path"].(string)
3077			return s.mcpReadDocument(workingDir, path)
3078		case "ddx_search":
3079			query, _ := call.Arguments["query"].(string)
3080			return s.mcpSearch(workingDir, query)
3081		case "ddx_resolve_persona":
3082			role, _ := call.Arguments["role"].(string)
3083			return s.mcpResolvePersona(workingDir, role)
3084		case "ddx_list_beads":
3085			status, _ := call.Arguments["status"].(string)
3086			label, _ := call.Arguments["label"].(string)
3087			return s.mcpListBeads(workingDir, status, label)
3088		case "ddx_show_bead":

3093		case "ddx_bead_status":

3097		case "ddx_doc_stale":

3099		case "ddx_doc_show":

7	# Feature: DDx Agent Service (consumer of ddx-agent contract)
8	
9	**ID:** FEAT-006
10	**Status:** In Progress (migrating to ddx-agent CONTRACT-003)
11	**Priority:** P0
12	**Owner:** DDx Team
13	
14	## Overview
15	
16	DDx invokes LLMs through the `ddx-agent` module, defined by
17	**CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service** in the `~/Projects/agent` repo
18	(`docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md`).
19	
20	That contract is the entire boundary. DDx's CLI commands (`ddx agent run`,
21	`ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx work`, `ddx agent providers`, `ddx agent models`,
22	`ddx agent route-status`, `ddx agent check`) are thin wrappers that call into
23	the `agentlib.DdxAgent` interface and render its results.
24	
25	## DDx-side responsibilities
26	
27	DDx owns the bead-driven workflow surface. It does NOT own:
28	- Harness orchestration (claude, codex, opencode, pi, gemini, native)
29	- Provider routing
30	- Model catalog
31	- Compaction
32	- Tool registration
33	- Session-log shape
34	
35	Those all live inside ddx-agent per CONTRACT-003.
36	
37	DDx owns:
38	
39	- **Bead-driven invocation.** `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx work`, and the
40	  server's queue-drain worker translate bead state into `ExecuteRequest`
41	  values and surface results back into the bead tracker.
42	- **Execute-bead orchestration.** Worktree creation, base-revision pinning,
43	  result landing (merge / preserve / no-changes), gate evaluation, evidence
44	  bundle capture. The agent provides the LLM execution; DDx provides the
45	  git-aware orchestration.
46	- **Evidence and session capture.** DDx writes `.ddx/executions/<id>/`
47	  bundles with prompts, manifests, and result artifacts. The agent's session
48	  log path (returned in `ExecuteResponse.SessionLogPath`) is captured into
49	  the bundle.
50	- **Profile policy at the request level.** DDx selects `Profile`, `Effort`,
51	  `Permissions` per bead (or per CLI invocation) based on bead metadata
52	  and user input. The agent receives those as `ExecuteRequest` fields and
53	  performs the routing.
54	
55	## Profile Routing
56	
57	`ddx work --profile default` is the primary queue-drain invocation. DDx maps
58	the user-facing profile to an ordered tier ladder and resolves each tier through
59	the agent service with the configured provider affinity and capability gating.
60	
61	| Profile | Ladder | Intent |
62	| --- | --- | --- |
63	| `default` | `[cheap, standard, smart]` | Local first, then subscription cloud, then high-quality cloud when earlier tiers fail. This is the common throughput-per-dollar path. |
64	| `cheap` | `[cheap]` | Local only. Never escalates; if local cannot serve the bead, the bead is unclaimed and waits. |
65	| `fast` | `[fast, smart]` | Cloud-fast first, skipping local warmup; escalates to smart on failure. |
66	| `smart` | `[smart]` | High-quality cloud from the start. No escalation. |
67	
68	The `.ddx/config.yaml` field `agent.routing.profile_ladders` can override the
69	ordered tier list per profile. The legacy flat `agent.routing.profile_priority`
70	is still read as the `default` profile fallback and emits a deprecation warning;
71	new configs should use `profile_ladders.default`. `agent.routing.model_overrides`
72	can map a ladder tier such as `cheap`, `standard`, `fast`, or `smart` to a
73	concrete model reference before DDx asks the agent service to resolve the route.
74	
75	Escalation advances to the next tier for `execution_failed`,
76	`land_conflict`, `post_run_check_failed`, and
77	`structural_validation_failed`. `no_changes` keeps the existing cooldown and
78	satisfaction-adjudication path rather than consuming a higher tier.
79	
80	### Conflict-recovery structured outcomes (bead ddx-0097af14)
81	
82	Before parking a bead on `land_conflict` (or on `execution_failed` when the
83	agent produced a commit before failing), the execute-loop now attempts to reuse
84	the preserved iteration ref rather than discarding the agent's work:
85	
86	1. **3-way ort auto-merge** (`git merge --no-ff -s ort -X ours`). If clean,
87	   the bead closes as `success`.
88	2. **Focused conflict-resolve agent** (if `ConflictResolver` is configured on
89	   the worker). If clean, the bead closes as `success`.
90	3. **Park with structured outcome.** Two new statuses replace the generic
91	   reopen when both recovery paths fail:
92	
93	| Status | Event kind | Meaning |
94	| --- | --- | --- |
95	| `land_conflict_unresolvable` | `land-conflict-unresolvable` | All auto-recovery failed; retryable after 15-min cooldown. |
96	| `land_conflict_needs_human` | `land-conflict-needs-human` | Focused-resolve agent returned BLOCKING; human input required. |
97	
98	Both statuses use `LandConflictCooldown` (15 min) rather than the 24h cap used
99	for `push_failed`, because land conflicts typically unblock quickly as sibling
100	beads advance the base branch.
101	
102	## Asking ddx-agent for changes
103	
104	When DDx needs new behavior from the agent — a new method, a new field on
105	`ExecuteRequest`, a new event type, a new policy knob — file a PR against
106	CONTRACT-003 in the agent repo. Maintainers decide whether the surface grows.
107	
108	Do not import agent internal packages. They live under `internal/` and the
109	Go compiler blocks external imports after agent v0.5.0 ships.
110	
111	## Migration status
112	
113	DDx is currently migrating from the legacy DDx-internal routing/harness
114	implementation to the CONTRACT-003 surface. Migration epic: `ddx-ac5c7fdb`.
115	Pre-migration, FEAT-006 documented harness orchestration responsibilities
116	that have since moved upstream.
117	
118	## Design References
119	
120	- `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-006-agent-session-capture.md` — session log shape and evidence bundles
121	- `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-013-multi-agent-coordination.md` — worktree-aware dispatch, concurrent bead safety
122	- `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-014-token-awareness.md` — provider availability, quota, and signal model
123	- `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-016-process-metrics.md` — bead-level cost and rework derivation
124	- `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md` — host+user coordinator scoping
125	- `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-021-service-backed-multi-node-topology.md` — service-backed node topology
126	- `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-006-agent-session-capture.md` — session log and artifact format
127	- `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md` — execution attempt bundles at `.ddx/executions/`
128	- `docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md` — current thin-consumer migration plan
129	- `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-006-agent-session-capture.md` — session capture test coverage
130	- `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-014-token-awareness.md` — token-awareness coverage
131	- `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-020-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md` — routing and catalog resolution coverage
132

7	# Feature: Document Dependency Graph
8	
9	**ID:** FEAT-007
10	**Status:** Complete
11	**Priority:** P0
12	**Owner:** DDx Team
13	
14	## Overview
15	
16	DDx tracks dependencies between documents using YAML frontmatter metadata. When an upstream document changes, DDx detects which downstream documents are stale and need review. This is the "keep documents honest" capability — the infrastructure that prevents document drift.
17	
18	Workflow tools and check runners consume the graph to enforce document quality. DDx owns the graph model, staleness detection, hashing, and stamping. Check runners delegate graph operations to DDx rather than implementing their own.
19	
20	## Problem Statement
21	
22	**Current situation:** Dun implements document dependency tracking internally (`doc_dag.go`, `frontmatter.go`, `hash.go`, `stamp.go`). This is document infrastructure, not check logic — it belongs in DDx alongside the document library it operates on.
23	
24	**Pain points:**
25	- Document staleness detection is locked inside the check runner, inaccessible to other tools
26	- No CLI command to inspect the document graph, check staleness, or stamp documents
27	- No MCP endpoint for agents to query document relationships
28	- The `dun:` frontmatter prefix ties a generic concept to a specific tool
29	
30	**Desired outcome:** DDx owns the document graph as a first-class service. Any tool can query it. The `ddx:` frontmatter convention is the standard way to declare document identity and dependencies.
31	
32	## The `ddx:` Frontmatter Convention
33	
34	Documents declare their identity and dependencies using YAML frontmatter with the `ddx:` namespace. GitHub hides YAML frontmatter in its markdown viewer, so this is invisible to casual readers.
35	
36	### Format
37	
38	```yaml
39	---
40	ddx:
41	  id: helix.architecture
42	  depends_on:
43	    - helix.prd
44	    - helix.feature-registry
45	  inputs:
46	    - node:helix.prd
47	    - refs:helix.prd
48	  review:
49	    self_hash: "a3f2dd..."
50	    deps:
51	      helix.prd: "7c6f43..."
52	    reviewed_at: "2026-04-03T12:00:00Z"
53	---
54	# Architecture Document
55	
56	Content here...
57	```
58	
59	### Fields
60	
61	| Field | Type | Required | Description |
62	|-------|------|----------|-------------|
63	| `id` | string | yes | Unique document identifier (e.g., `helix.prd`, `SD-001`) |
64	| `depends_on` | []string | no | IDs of documents this one depends on |
65	| `inputs` | []string | no | Input selectors for prompt context resolution |
66	| `review` | object | no | Staleness tracking metadata (managed by `ddx doc stamp`) |
67	| `review.self_hash` | string | auto | Content hash of this document (excluding review block) |
68	| `review.deps` | map[string]string | auto | Map of dependency ID → hash at last review |
69	| `review.reviewed_at` | string | auto | ISO-8601 timestamp of last stamp |
70	| `prompt` | string | no | Custom prompt for updating this document |
71	| `parking_lot` | bool | no | Skip this document in staleness checks |
72	
73	### Compatibility
74	
75	- The `ddx:` prefix replaces the existing `dun:` prefix. DDx should read both `ddx:` and `dun:` frontmatter for backward compatibility, but write `ddx:`.
76	- Documents without `ddx:` frontmatter are ignored by the graph.
77	- Unknown fields within the `ddx:` block are preserved on round-trip.
78	
79	## Requirements
80	
81	### Functional
82	
83	1. **Frontmatter parsing** — read `ddx:` (and legacy `dun:`) YAML frontmatter from markdown files
84	2. **Graph construction** — scan a directory tree, parse frontmatter, build a directed acyclic graph of document dependencies
85	3. **Content hashing** — deterministic hash of document content excluding the `review` block. Same content = same hash always.
86	4. **Staleness detection** — a document is stale when any dependency's current hash differs from the hash recorded in `review.deps`
87	5. **Cascade propagation** — if a document is stale, all its dependents are transitively stale
88	6. **Stamp command** (`ddx doc stamp`) — update `review.self_hash` and `review.deps` to mark a document as reviewed against its current dependencies
89	7. **Graph query** (`ddx doc graph`) — show the document dependency graph as text or JSON
90	8. **Stale query** (`ddx doc stale`) — list stale documents
91	9. **Input resolution** — resolve `inputs` selectors (`node:`, `refs:`, `code_refs:`, `paths:`) to actual content for prompt assembly
92	10. **Graph configuration** — optional `.ddx/graphs/*.yaml` files defining required roots, ID-to-path mappings, and cascade rules
93	11. **Body-link indexing** — scan document bodies for `[[ID]]` reference syntax and index those as graph edges alongside frontmatter-declared `depends_on` edges. Support plain IDs (`[[FEAT-001]]`), slugged IDs (`[[US-036-list-mcp-servers]]`), and dotted IDs (`[[helix.workflow.artifact-hierarchy]]`). Return the union of body links and frontmatter edges without duplicate edges.
94	
95	### Non-Functional
96	
97	- **Performance:** Graph construction <500ms for repos with 100+ documents
98	- **Determinism:** Same document content always produces the same hash
99	- **Portability:** Works on any repo with markdown files containing `ddx:` frontmatter
100	- **Backward compatibility:** Reads `dun:` frontmatter, writes `ddx:`
101	
102	## CLI Commands
103	
104	```bash
105	ddx doc graph [--json]              # Show document dependency graph
106	ddx doc stale [--json]              # List stale documents
107	ddx doc stamp [paths...] [--all]    # Update review stamps
108	ddx doc show <id>                   # Show document metadata and status
109	ddx doc deps <id>                   # Show what a document depends on
110	ddx doc dependents <id>             # Show what depends on a document
111	```
112	
113	## Server Endpoints (FEAT-002 integration)
114	
115	| MCP Tool | HTTP Endpoint | Description |
116	|----------|--------------|-------------|
117	| `ddx_doc_graph` | `GET /api/docs/graph` | Full dependency graph |
118	| `ddx_doc_stale` | `GET /api/docs/stale` | List stale documents |
119	| `ddx_doc_show` | `GET /api/docs/:id` | Document metadata and status |
120	| `ddx_doc_deps` | `GET /api/docs/:id/deps` | Dependencies of a document |
121	
122	## User Stories
123	
124	### US-070: Developer Checks Document Freshness
125	**As a** developer maintaining project docs
126	**I want** to see which documents are stale after upstream changes
127	**So that** I know what needs reviewing
128	
129	**Acceptance Criteria:**
130	- Given the PRD has changed since the architecture doc was last stamped, when I run `ddx doc stale`, then the architecture doc is listed as stale
131	- Given I update the architecture doc and run `ddx doc stamp docs/architecture.md`, then it's no longer reported as stale
132	
133	### US-071: Agent Discovers Document Relationships
134	**As an** AI agent working on a feature
135	**I want** to query which documents govern this area of the codebase
136	**So that** I can load the right context
137	
138	**Acceptance Criteria:**
139	- Given ddx-server is running, when an agent calls `ddx_doc_deps` with a design doc ID, then it receives the list of upstream governing documents
140	- Given an agent calls `ddx_doc_stale`, then it knows which documents need attention before proceeding
141	
142	### US-073: Graph Indexes Body Links and Supports Reverse Traversal
143	**As** DDx (and tools consuming the graph)
144	**I want** document body `[[ID]]` references indexed as graph edges
145	**So that** dependency relationships declared in prose are machine-queryable without grep
146	
147	**Acceptance Criteria:**
148	- Given a markdown document containing `[[FEAT-011]]`, `[[US-036-list-mcp-servers]]`, and `[[helix.workflow.artifact-hierarchy]]` in its body, when DDx constructs the graph, then those references are indexed as resolvable graph edges.
149	- Given both `ddx.depends_on: [A]` frontmatter and a `[[A]]` body reference in the same document, when the graph is queried, then a single edge to A is returned — not two.
150	- Given documents B and C each contain `[[A]]` in their bodies, when dependents of A are queried, then B and C are returned from the graph index without requiring an external file scan.
151	- Given an execution document declares `ddx.depends_on: [FEAT-001]`, when the graph is queried, then the execution document is returned as a discoverable graph artifact linked to FEAT-001.
152	- Given malformed `[[...]]` syntax (e.g., `[[]]`, `[[has spaces]]`) or an `[[UnknownID]]` reference, when the graph is constructed, then the reference is skipped silently and graph construction completes without error.
153	
154	### US-072: Check Runner Delegates Graph Operations
155	**As** dun (check runner)
156	**I want** to call `ddx doc stale` instead of implementing my own graph logic
157	**So that** document staleness is a shared service
158	
159	**Acceptance Criteria:**
160	- Given dun's doc-dag check is configured, when it runs, then it calls `ddx doc stale --json` and reports the results
161	- Given dun's change-cascade check detects upstream changes, then it calls `ddx doc stale` to identify affected downstream documents
162	
163	## Implementation Notes
164	
165	### Porting from Dun
166	
167	The following dun source files contain the logic to port:
168	
169	| Dun File | What It Does | DDx Destination |
170	|----------|-------------|----------------|
171	| `internal/dun/frontmatter.go` | Parse/write `dun:` frontmatter | `internal/docgraph/frontmatter.go` |
172	| `internal/dun/doc_dag.go` | Build graph, detect staleness, cascade | `internal/docgraph/graph.go` |
173	| `internal/dun/hash.go` | Deterministic content hashing | `internal/docgraph/hash.go` |
174	| `internal/dun/stamp.go` | Update review stamps in frontmatter | `internal/docgraph/stamp.go` |
175	
176	### Migration Strategy
177	
178	**Phase 1:** DDx ships doc graph commands (`ddx doc graph/stale/stamp`). Reads both `ddx:` and `dun:` frontmatter, writes `ddx:`.
179	
180	**Phase 2:** Dun adds `ddx doc stale --json` as alternative to internal graph logic, gated by `DUN_USE_DDX_DOC=1`.
181	
182	**Phase 3:** Once proven, dun removes internal doc_dag/frontmatter/hash/stamp code and delegates fully to DDx.
183	
184	**Phase 4:** A one-time migration tool converts `dun:` frontmatter to `ddx:` in existing repos.
185	
186	## Edge Cases
187	
188	- Document with `ddx:` frontmatter but no `id` — skip, warn
189	- Circular dependencies — detect and report, don't infinite loop
190	- Missing dependency (ID referenced but document not found) — report as warning, don't fail
191	- Mixed `dun:` and `ddx:` in same repo — read both, prefer `ddx:` if both present on same file
192	- Binary files or non-markdown — skip silently
193	- Very large repos (1000+ markdown files) — incremental scanning with caching
194	
195	## Dependencies
196	
197	- DDx CLI infrastructure (config, command factory)
198	- Markdown files with `ddx:` frontmatter
199	- For server endpoints: FEAT-002
200	
201	## Execution Documents
202	
203	Execution documents are a category of graph-discovered artifact that declare
204	what validations, checks, and measurements should run for a bead or set of
205	governing artifacts. They live in the worktree as ordinary markdown files with
206	`ddx:` frontmatter and participate in DDx document indexing alongside other
207	graph artifacts.
208	
209	### Frontmatter convention
210	
211	```yaml
212	---
213	ddx:
214	  id: exec.FEAT-001.acceptance-smoke
215	  depends_on:
216	    - FEAT-001
217	  execution:
218	    kind: command             # command | agent
219	    required: true            # true = merge-blocking in execute-bead
220	    command: ["make", "test"]
221	    cwd: cli
222	    timeout_ms: 120000
223	---
224	# Acceptance Smoke Test for FEAT-001
225	...
226	```
227	
228	### Key fields
229	
230	| Field | Description |
231	|-------|-------------|
232	| `ddx.id` | Unique document ID; by convention prefixed `exec.` |
233	| `ddx.depends_on` | Governing artifacts this execution is linked to |
234	| `ddx.execution.kind` | Executor kind: `command` or `agent` |
235	| `ddx.execution.required` | `true` means this execution is merge-blocking in `execute-bead` |
236	
237	### Discovery
238	
239	`ddx agent execute-bead` resolves applicable execution documents from the graph
240	inside the execution worktree by following dependency links from the target bead
241	and its governing artifacts. DDx indexes execution documents like other graph
242	artifacts; no separate registry is required.
243	
244	### Relationship to FEAT-010
245	
246	Execution documents are the git-backed, authored source of truth for what
247	`ddx exec` runs. FEAT-010's exec substrate stores immutable run history.
248	FEAT-007's graph owns the discovery and indexing of execution document
249	definitions.
250	
251	## Git-Aware History (FEAT-012 Integration)
252	
253	The following commands extend the doc graph with revision-control-aware
254	queries. They are defined in FEAT-012 but operate on the document graph:
255	
256	```bash
257	ddx doc history <id> [--since <ref>]   # Commit log for an artifact
258	ddx doc diff <id> [<ref1>] [<ref2>]    # Content diff between refs
259	ddx doc changed [--since <ref>]        # Artifacts changed since a ref
260	```
261	
262	These require the graph's artifact-ID-to-file-path mapping and are
263	surfaced alongside the existing graph/stale/stamp commands.
264	
265	## Out of Scope
266	
267	- Code-to-document tracing (which code implements which spec) — that's dun's `spec-binding` check
268	- Automated document updating — DDx detects staleness, agents do the updating
269	- Document content validation (correct sections, completeness) — that's workflow-level
270	- Branch management, remote operations, merge conflict resolution (see FEAT-012 Out of Scope)
271	- **Graph traversal policy** — authority ordering, impact-flow strategy, and search fallback policy are delegated to workflow tools; DDx provides the graph primitives (edges, dependents, staleness), not the traversal strategy
272	- **Artifact decomposition policy** — how to break a change across an artifact stack is a plugin concern; DDx indexes the artifacts and their relationships
273

10	# Feature: Multi-Node Dashboard UI
11	
12	**ID:** FEAT-021
13	**Status:** Complete
14	**Priority:** P1
15	**Owner:** DDx Team
16	
17	## Overview
18	
19	Extends the FEAT-008 web UI with a node/project-aware dashboard. The UI can
20	show a combined view of beads and agent sessions across all registered projects
21	on a node, or narrow to a specific project for context-dependent views
22	(document browser, dependency graph, commit log). Node, project, and page are
23	all embedded in the URL so every view is bookmarkable and shareable.
24	
25	The dashboard UI is implemented as a SvelteKit application with graphql-request for
26	GraphQL data fetching. Every page uses `+page.ts` load functions to fetch data
27	from the `/graphql` endpoint defined in SD-022.
28	
29	## Problem Statement
30	
31	**Current situation:** The FEAT-008 UI is implicitly scoped to the single
32	project the server was started in. There is no node concept, no project picker,
33	and no combined cross-project view for beads or agent sessions.
34	
35	**Pain points:**
36	- Operator cannot see all active work across projects in one dashboard
37	- Navigating to a different project requires restarting the server in that
38	  project's directory
39	- Deep links to a specific bead or document are not stable because the URL
40	  carries no project context
41	- Node identity is invisible in the UI — there is no indication of which
42	  machine or server instance is being viewed
43	
44	**Desired outcome:** A single `ddx server` UI that surfaces everything
45	happening on the node. The operator can scan the full bead queue and agent
46	session log across all projects, drill into a specific project for
47	document-level work, and bookmark any view with a stable URL.
48	
49	## URL Structure
50	
51	All routes embed context so every view is directly addressable:
52	
53	```
54	/                                     → redirect to /nodes/:nodeId
55	/nodes/:nodeId                        → node overview (health, project list)
56	/nodes/:nodeId/sessions               → combined agent sessions (all projects)
57	/nodes/:nodeId/beads                  → combined bead queue (all projects)
58	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId                   → project overview
59	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/beads             → project beads
60	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/documents         → document browser
61	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/graph             → doc dependency graph
62	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/commits           → commit log
63	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/sessions          → project agent sessions
64	```
65	
66	**Combined views** (`/nodes/:nodeId/sessions`, `/nodes/:nodeId/beads`) operate
67	across all registered projects. They call the existing `/api/agent/sessions`
68	and `/api/beads` endpoints which are already node-scoped (since one server =
69	one node). A future multi-node coordinator would fan these out across nodes.
70	
71	**Project-scoped views** (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/...`) pass the
72	project ID to the existing `/api/projects/:project/...` API routes defined in
73	SD-019. The UI must always resolve which project context to bind before
74	making API calls.
75	
76	The `:nodeId` segment is the stable node ID from `GET /api/node` (e.g.
77	`node-7029e8d6`). The `:projectId` segment is the stable project ID from
78	`GET /api/projects` (e.g. `proj-96d7ea83`).
79	
80	## Views
81	
82	### Node Overview (`/nodes/:nodeId`)
83	
84	- Node name, ID, and uptime
85	- Health summary cards: library status, bead store, doc graph per project
86	- Project list with last-seen timestamp and health badge
87	- Quick links to combined sessions and combined beads
88	- Server version and started_at
89	
90	**GraphQL Query:** `node` query from FEAT-020, `projects` query for project list.
91	
92	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/+page.svelte`
93	
94	### Combined Bead Queue (`/nodes/:nodeId/beads`)
95

308	## Implementation Notes
309	
310	### Router
311	
312	SvelteKit handles routing automatically based on `src/routes/` file structure.
313	Routes are nested using the folder hierarchy:
314	
315	```
316	src/routes/
317	├── +layout.svelte              → Node+Project layout with nav
318	├── +page.ts                    → Root redirect to /nodes/:nodeId
319	└── nodes/
320	    └── [$nodeId]/
321	        ├── +layout.ts          → Load node context
322	        ├── +page.svelte        → NodeOverview
323	        ├── beads/              → CombinedBeads
324	        │   └── +page.svelte
325	        ├── sessions/           → CombinedSessions
326	        │   └── +page.svelte
327	        └── projects/
328	            └── [$projectId]/
329	                ├── +page.svelte      → ProjectOverview
330	                ├── beads/            → ProjectBeads
331	                │   └── +page.svelte
332	                ├── documents/        → DocumentBrowser
333	                │   └── +page.svelte
334	                ├── graph/            → DocGraph
335	                │   └── +page.svelte
336	                ├── commits/          → CommitLog
337	                │   └── +page.svelte
338	                └── sessions/         → ProjectSessions
339	                    └── +page.svelte
340	```
341	
342	The `[$nodeId]` and `[$projectId]` dynamic route segments capture URL parameters.
343	SvelteKit's `+layout.ts` and `+page.ts` files define load functions that
344	fetch data via graphql-request.
345	
346	### Data Layer
347	
348	graphql-request is the GraphQL client for data fetching, with graphql-ws for
349	subscriptions. Pages define queries inline using the `gql` tagged template
350	and manually typed response interfaces in `+page.ts` load functions.
351	
352	Example bead list query:
353	
354	```typescript
355	// src/routes/nodes/$nodeId/beads/+page.ts
356	import { gql } from 'graphql-request';
357	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client';
358	
359	const BEAD_LIST_QUERY = gql`
360	  query BeadList($projectID: ID) {
361	    beads(projectID: $projectID, first: 50) {
362	      edges {
363	        node {
364	          id
365	          title
366	          status
367	          priority
368	          projectID
369	        }
370	      }
371	    }
372	  }
373	`;
374	
375	export const load = async ({ fetch, params }) => {
376	  const client = createClient(fetch);
377	  const data = await client.request(BEAD_LIST_QUERY, { projectID: params.projectId });
378	  return { beads: data.beads };
379	};
380	```
381	
382	graphql-ws subscription support enables real-time updates without polling.
383	
384	### Project Context
385	
386	The `ProjectStore` (using Svelte 5 runes) stores the selected node ID and
387	project ID. It's populated from URL params by `+layout.ts` load functions:
388	
389	```typescript
390	// src/lib/stores/project.ts
391	import { writable } from 'svelte/store';
392	
393	export const projectStore = writable<{
394	  nodeId: string;
395	  projectId?: string;
396	} | null>(null);
397	```
398	
399	Components read from `projectStore` to construct API URLs and filter queries.

// Routing test seams (ddx-755f5881 AC #2): record every RouteRequest issued
// by the default `ddx agent run` path so unit tests can assert that the
// default path calls ResolveRoute exactly once with Profile: "default" and
// no other fields. Production code never reads this slice; it is exported
// only for tests.
var (
	routeRequestRecorderMu sync.Mutex
	routeRequestRecorder   []agentlib.RouteRequest
)

func recordRouteRequestForTest(req agentlib.RouteRequest) {
	routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
	routeRequestRecorder = append(routeRequestRecorder, req)
	routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
}

// RecordedRouteRequestsForTest returns a copy of all RouteRequests recorded
// by the agent CLI surface since the last reset.
func RecordedRouteRequestsForTest() []agentlib.RouteRequest {
	routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
	defer routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
	out := make([]agentlib.RouteRequest, len(routeRequestRecorder))
	copy(out, routeRequestRecorder)
	return out
}

// ResetRecordedRouteRequestsForTest clears the route-request recording slice.
func ResetRecordedRouteRequestsForTest() {
	routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
	routeRequestRecorder = nil
	routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
}

12	# Feature: DDx Server
13	
14	**ID:** FEAT-002
15	**Status:** Complete
16	**Priority:** P0
17	**Owner:** DDx Team
18	
19	## Overview
20	
21	`ddx-server` is a lightweight Go web server that exposes DDx platform services
22	over HTTP and MCP endpoints. It serves documents, beads, execution definitions
23	and run history, the document dependency graph, DDx agent invocation activity
24	plus embedded-agent telemetry references, and (via FEAT-008) an embedded web
25	UI — all from a single binary.
26	
27	`ddx-server` runs as a per-user host daemon: one server process per machine,
28	scoped to one operating-system user. It holds its identity and project
29	registry in user-level state — not inside any project — and supervises
30	execute-loop workers for every project it has registered. The state file lives
31	at `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json` (`$XDG_DATA_HOME/ddx/server-state.json`
32	when set), and the last-known server URL is written to
33	`~/.local/share/ddx/server.addr` (see FEAT-020). The server binds multiple
34	project roots concurrently; each request is resolved against one explicit
35	project context before adapters run.
36	
37	## Architecture
38	
39	```
40	ddx-server binary
41	├── /projects  → Project registry / project picker
42	├── /            → Web UI (embedded SPA, FEAT-008)
43	├── /api/        → HTTP REST API (JSON)
44	├── /graphql     → GraphQL API (SvelteKit frontend queries)
45	└── /mcp/        → MCP tool endpoints (Streamable HTTP transport)
46	```
47	
48	All four surfaces share the same underlying services. The SvelteKit frontend queries the server via GraphQL. MCP tools call the same service layer.
49	
50	### Project Registry and Routing
51	
52	`ddx-server` resolves every request against an explicit project context
53	before dispatching to feature-specific adapters.
54	
55	**Registry contract**
56	
57	- the server holds one project registry per host+user, persisted at
58	  `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json` as specified in FEAT-020
59	- projects enter the registry by auto-registration from CLI commands
60	  (`ddx bead`, `ddx agent`, `ddx doc`) running in any project directory on
61	  the machine, and by the server registering its own startup working directory
62	- optional seed entries may be provided via `server.projects` in config so a
63	  fresh install can boot with a known project list before any CLI commands run
64	- each entry carries a stable `id`, an absolute `root`, an optional display
65	  `name`, a `git_remote`, and `registered_at`/`last_seen` timestamps
66	- if the state file is absent, the server synthesizes a singleton project from
67	  the current working directory so today's `ddx server` invocation still works
68	
69	**Canonical project-scoped surfaces**
70	
71	- `GET /api/projects` - list configured project roots and health
72	- `GET /api/projects/:project` - show one project context
73	- `ddx_list_projects` - enumerate projects over MCP
74	- `ddx_show_project` - show one project context over MCP
75	
76	**Selection rules**
77	
78	1. explicit project in the request path
79	2. explicit project supplied by the caller
80	3. configured default project
81	4. implicit singleton project when only one project exists
82	
83	Legacy unscoped `/api/...` and `/mcp/...` forms remain only as compatibility aliases when the server can resolve exactly one project context. They are not the canonical multi-project contract.
84	
85	### Worker Boundaries
86	
87	`ddx-server` hosts an in-process `WorkerManager` that supervises
88	execute-loop workers as goroutines. Each execute-loop worker runs a
89	`ddx agent execute-loop` against exactly one registered project context; it
90	never crosses project boundaries. Worker lifecycle (start, live logs, stop,
91	record on disk) is owned by the server, so the host+user daemon is the single
92	point of coordination for all long-running agent activity on the machine. The
93	supervisor exposes worker state through the same project-scoped API surface
94	used for beads and executions, and worker records persist under the project's
95	own `.ddx/workers/` directory so preservation and cleanup stay scoped per
96	project.
97	
98	When multiple machines each run `ddx-server` against the same project, each
99	machine runs its own land coordinator against its local clone. The shared git
100	remote is the only coordination point between machines: a `git push --ff-only`

- `ddx_resolve_persona` - Resolve the persona bound to a role

**Bead (Work Item) Tools:**
- `ddx_list_beads` - List work items with optional filters (status, label)
- `ddx_show_bead` - Show details of specific bead
- `ddx_bead_ready` - List ready beads (open with all dependencies closed)
- `ddx_bead_status` - Get bead summary counts by status
- `ddx_bead_create` - Create new bead (title, type, priority, labels, description, acceptance)
- `ddx_bead_update` - Update bead fields (status, labels, description, acceptance)
- `ddx_bead_claim` - Claim bead for current session (sets status to in_progress)

**Document Graph Tools:**
- `ddx_doc_graph` - Get full document dependency graph
- `ddx_doc_stale` - List stale documents
- `ddx_doc_show` - Show document metadata and staleness
- `ddx_doc_deps` - Get upstream dependencies of a document
- `ddx_doc_changed` - List artifacts changed since a git ref
- `ddx_doc_write` - Write content to document by artifact ID
- `ddx_doc_history` - Get git commit history for document
- `ddx_doc_diff` - Get git diff for document

**Agent Session & Provider Tools:**
- `ddx_agent_sessions` - List recent agent sessions (filterable by harness)
- `ddx_provider_list` - List all configured provider harnesses with routing availability, auth state, quota/headroom, signal freshness (host+user global, not project-scoped)
- `ddx_provider_show` - Get full routing signal snapshot for one provider harness (FEAT-014 read model)

**Execution Tools:**
- `ddx_exec_definitions` - List execution definitions with optional artifact filter
- `ddx_exec_show` - Show specific execution definition
- `ddx_exec_history` - List execution runs (filterable by artifact and definition)
- `ddx_exec_dispatch` - Dispatch execution run by definition ID (localhost-only)

**Agent Dispatch Tools:**
- `ddx_agent_dispatch` - Dispatch agent invocation (localhost-only) with harness, prompt, model, effort, project params

**Project Tools:**
- `ddx_list_projects` - List projects registered with ddx-server node
- `ddx_show_project` - Show registered project by ID or path

---

### 2. FEAT-002 (Server) - HTTP/MCP Coverage

**File:** `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-002-server.md`

FEAT-002 defines the DDx Server as a lightweight Go web server exposing platform services over HTTP and MCP endpoints. The server:
- Runs as per-user host daemon (one per machine per OS user)
- Holds identity and project registry in user-level state
- Supervises execute-loop workers for every registered project
- State file: `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`
- Serves four API surfaces: Project Registry, Web UI (FEAT-008), HTTP REST API, GraphQL API, and MCP tool endpoints
- All surfaces share same underlying services
- MCP tools use Streamable HTTP transport

**Project Registry:**
- `GET /api/projects` - list configured project roots
- `GET /api/projects/:project` - show one project
- MCP: `ddx_list_projects`, `ddx_show_project`

Auto-registration occurs from CLI commands; optional seed entries via `server.projects` in config.

---

### 3. FEAT-006 (Agent Service) - HTTP/MCP Surfaces

**File:** `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`

FEAT-006 documents DDx's invocation of LLMs through the `ddx-agent` module (CONTRACT-003). The contract is the entire boundary. DDx CLI commands:
- `ddx agent run`
- `ddx agent execute-bead`
- `ddx work`
- `ddx agent providers`
- `ddx agent models`
- `ddx agent route-status`
- `ddx agent check`

These are thin wrappers calling the `agentlib.DdxAgent` interface.

**DDx Owns:**
- Bead-driven invocation
- Execute-bead orchestration (worktree creation, base-revision pinning, result landing, gate evaluation, evidence bundle capture)
- Evidence and session capture (`.ddx/executions/<id>/` bundles)
- Profile policy at request level (Profile, Effort, Permissions per bead)

**Profile Routing:**
| Profile | Ladder | Intent |
|---------|--------|--------|
| `default` | `[cheap, standard, smart]` | Local first → cloud → high-quality |
| `cheap` | `[cheap]` | Local only, no escalation |
| `fast` | `[fast, smart]` | Cloud-fast first → smart |
| `smart` | `[smart]` | High-quality cloud, no escalation |

Configuration via `.ddx/config.yaml`: `agent.routing.profile_ladders`, legacy `agent.routing.profile_priority`, `agent.routing.model_overrides`.

---

### 4. FEAT-007 (Document Dependency Graph) - MCP Tools

**File:** `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-007-doc-graph.md`

FEAT-007 tracks document dependencies via YAML frontmatter metadata. MCP tools planned:

| MCP Tool | HTTP Endpoint | Description |
|----------|--------------|-------------|
| `ddx_doc_graph` | `GET /api/docs/graph` | Full dependency graph |
| `ddx_doc_stale` | `GET /api/docs/stale` | List stale documents |
| `ddx_doc_show` | `GET /api/docs/:id` | Document metadata and status |
| `ddx_doc_deps` | `GET /api/docs/:id/deps` | Dependencies of a document |

CLI Commands:
- `ddx doc graph [--json]`
- `ddx doc stale [--json]`
- `ddx doc stamp [paths...] [--all]`
- `ddx doc show <id>`
- `ddx doc deps <id>`
- `ddx doc dependents <id>`

**Frontmatter Convention:** Documents use `ddx:` YAML block with:
- `id`: Unique document identifier
- `depends_on`: List of document IDs
- `inputs`: Input selectors for prompt context
- `review`: Staleness tracking (self_hash, deps map, reviewed_at)
- `prompt`: Custom prompt for updating
- `parking_lot`: Skip in staleness checks

Status: Complete. Supports both legacy `dun:` and new `ddx:` frontmatter.

**Body-link indexing:** Scans for `[[ID]]` reference syntax (plain IDs, slugged IDs like `[[US-036-list-mcp-servers]]`, dotted IDs like `[[helix.workflow.artifact-hierarchy]]`).

---

### 5. FEAT-021 (Dashboard UI) - Run-History Routes

**File:** `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md`

FEAT-021 extends web UI with node/project-aware dashboard. URL structure and routes:

```
/                                     → redirect to /nodes/:nodeId
/nodes/:nodeId                        → node overview
/nodes/:nodeId/sessions               → combined agent sessions (all projects)
/nodes/:nodeId/beads                  → combined bead queue (all projects)

/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/graph             → doc dependency graph
/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/commits           → commit log
/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/sessions          → project agent sessions
```

**New API Required:**
- `GET /api/projects/:project/commits` - Git log for project; supports `?limit=&since=&author=`

All data served by existing FEAT-002, FEAT-004, FEAT-006, FEAT-008, SD-019 endpoints.

**Status:** Complete. Implementation uses SvelteKit with graphql-request for GraphQL data fetching. Node identity from `GET /api/node` (e.g., `node-7029e8d6`), project ID from `GET /api/projects` (e.g., `proj-96d7ea83`).

**File:** `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1/cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go`

# Read-Coverage Audit — HTTP/MCP Gap Analysis

**Date:** 2026-04-29
**Bead:** ddx-44236615 (artifact-run-arch read-coverage audit)
**Plan:** plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md § Bead #20

## Scope

Enumerate every CLI-visible read surface → map to HTTP REST and MCP tool coverage → identify gaps. Sequenced by user-visibility impact.

---

## Coverage Matrix

### Bead (Work Item) Reads

| CLI Command | HTTP Route | MCP Tool | Gap? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ddx bead list` | `GET /api/beads` | `ddx_list_beads` | — |
| `ddx bead show <id>` | `GET /api/beads/{id}` | `ddx_show_bead` | — |
| `ddx bead ready` | `GET /api/beads/ready` | `ddx_bead_ready` | — |
| `ddx bead status` | `GET /api/beads/status` | `ddx_bead_status` | — |
| `ddx bead blocked` | `GET /api/beads/blocked` | **MISSING** | **G3** |
| `ddx bead dep tree <id>` | `GET /api/beads/dep/tree/{id}` | **MISSING** | **G3** |
| `ddx bead evidence list <id>` | **MISSING** | **MISSING** | **G12** |
| `ddx bead cooldown show <id>` | **MISSING** | **MISSING** | **G12** |
| `ddx bead routing` | **MISSING** | **MISSING** | **G12** |

### Document Graph Reads

| CLI Command | HTTP Route | MCP Tool | Gap? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ddx doc graph` | `GET /api/docs/graph` | `ddx_doc_graph` | — |
| `ddx doc stale` | `GET /api/docs/stale` | `ddx_doc_stale` | — |
| `ddx doc show <id>` | `GET /api/docs/{id}` | `ddx_doc_show` | — |
| `ddx doc deps <id>` | `GET /api/docs/{id}/deps` | `ddx_doc_deps` | — |
| `ddx doc dependents <id>` | `GET /api/docs/{id}/dependents` | **MISSING** | **G1** |
| `ddx doc history <id>` | `GET /api/docs/{id}/history` | `ddx_doc_history` | — |
| `ddx doc diff <id>` | `GET /api/docs/{id}/diff` | `ddx_doc_diff` | — |
| `ddx doc changed` | **MISSING** | `ddx_doc_changed` | **G10** |
| `ddx doc audit` | (GraphQL `docGraphIssues`) | **MISSING** | minor |

### Agent Service Reads

| CLI Command | HTTP Route | MCP Tool | Gap? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ddx agent providers` | `GET /api/providers` | `ddx_provider_list` | — |
| `ddx agent route-status` | `GET /api/providers/{harness}` | `ddx_provider_show` | — |
| `ddx agent sessions` / `log` | `GET /api/agent/sessions` | `ddx_agent_sessions` | — |
| `ddx agent models` | **MISSING** | **MISSING** | **G5** |
| `ddx agent catalog show` | **MISSING** | **MISSING** | **G5** |
| `ddx agent capabilities` | **MISSING** | **MISSING** | **G5** |
| `ddx agent usage` | **MISSING** | **MISSING** | **G5** |
| `ddx agent workers` | `GET /api/agent/workers` | **MISSING** | **G4** |
| `ddx server workers list` | `GET /api/agent/workers` | **MISSING** | **G4** |
| `ddx server workers show <id>` | `GET /api/agent/workers/{id}` | **MISSING** | **G4** |
| `ddx server workers log <id>` | `GET /api/agent/workers/{id}/log` | **MISSING** | **G4** |

---

## Gap Catalog (sequenced by user-visibility impact)

### P1 — Core MCP parity: agent workflow blockers

**G1 · `ddx_doc_dependents` MCP tool** (FEAT-007)
- HTTP covers this: `GET /api/docs/{id}/dependents` exists
- MCP has `ddx_doc_deps` (upstream direction) but NOT the reverse (`dependents`)
- Impact: Agents doing cascade staleness analysis or "what breaks if I change X" can't perform reverse-dependency lookups via MCP. This is the most common agent reasoning pattern for document graphs.

**G2 · MCP exec run detail + log tools**
- HTTP covers: `GET /api/exec/runs/{id}` (result), `GET /api/exec/runs/{id}/log`
- MCP has `ddx_exec_history` (list runs) but no `ddx_exec_run` (single run) or `ddx_exec_run_log`
- Impact: Agents dispatch runs and then can't check results or logs via MCP without switching to HTTP. Breaks the MCP-only agent workflow feedback loop.

**G3 · `ddx_bead_blocked` + `ddx_bead_dep_tree` MCP tools**
- HTTP covers both: `GET /api/beads/blocked`, `GET /api/beads/dep/tree/{id}`
- MCP missing both
- Impact: Agents managing the bead queue via MCP can't check blocked state or inspect dependency trees. Required for agents doing queue drain planning or dependency-aware scheduling.

**G4 · MCP worker status tools**
- HTTP covers: `GET /api/agent/workers`, `GET /api/agent/workers/{id}`, `GET /api/agent/workers/{id}/log`
- MCP has no worker tools at all
- Impact: Agents coordinating or monitoring parallel workers can't query worker state. Affects multi-agent orchestration patterns.

### P2 — Configuration transparency

**G5 · Agent models / catalog / capabilities HTTP + MCP**
- CLI: `ddx agent models`, `ddx agent catalog show`, `ddx agent capabilities`, `ddx agent usage`
- HTTP: No route for models, catalog, or capabilities (providers/routing is covered)
- MCP: No tools
- Impact: High — there's no server-side way to discover available models, tier assignments, or capability metadata. Automated model selection and cost tiering depend on this. The endpoint-first routing redesign (memory: project_endpoint_routing_design) needs these surfaces.

**G6 · `ddx_list_personas` MCP tool**
- HTTP: `GET /api/personas` covers list
- MCP: `ddx_resolve_persona` resolves by role only; no list tool
- Impact: Agents selecting a persona for a task can't enumerate available options via MCP; must know the role name in advance.

### P3 — Process intelligence

**G7 · MCP process metrics tools**
- HTTP: `/api/metrics/{summary,cost,cycle-time,rework}` all exist
- MCP: No metrics tools at all
- Impact: Cost-aware agents (per cost-tiered-work standing goal) can't query process metrics via MCP without switching to HTTP. Affects FEAT-016 read parity.

**G8 · Layer-aware run-history HTTP + MCP** (FEAT-021 unified substrate)
- No routes exist for the three-layer substrate (`work` → `try` → `run` drill-down)
- Required for FEAT-021 layer-aware dashboard and for agents traversing the execution hierarchy
- Note: this gap will be partly addressed by the three-layer run substrate bead (plan #18); listed here to sequence the server-side read exposure.

**G9 · MCP server registry + plugin manifest HTTP + MCP**
- CLI: `ddx mcp list`, `ddx install installed/outdated/search`
- HTTP + MCP: No routes or tools
- Impact: Agents or UIs can't discover which MCP servers are configured or which plugins are installed without spawning the CLI. Needed for plugin-aware workflows.

### P4 — Completeness

**G10 · `GET /api/docs/changed` REST route**
- MCP has `ddx_doc_changed`; HTTP is missing this route
- Impact: Low — REST clients (not MCP) can't query changed artifacts. Easy add alongside FEAT-007 work.

**G11 · Per-metric-id history/trend HTTP + MCP**
- CLI: `ddx metric history <id>`, `ddx metric trend <id>`
- HTTP: Only aggregate endpoints; no per-metric-id time-series
- MCP: None
- Impact: Low-medium — time-series drill-down is used for trend analysis but not on the critical agent path.

**G12 · Bead evidence + cooldown + routing HTTP + MCP**
- CLI: `ddx bead evidence list <id>`, `ddx bead cooldown show <id>`, `ddx bead routing`
- HTTP + MCP: No coverage
- Impact: Low — operational details primarily used by the execute-loop itself; not on the agent-facing critical path.

---

## Per-Gap Bead List (proposed, sequenced by impact)

| Priority | Gap | Proposed Bead Title | FEAT ref |
|---|---|---|---|
| P1.1 | G1 | `[read-coverage] add ddx_doc_dependents MCP tool` | FEAT-007 |
| P1.2 | G2 | `[read-coverage] add ddx_exec_run + ddx_exec_run_log MCP tools` | FEAT-002/010 |
| P1.3 | G3 | `[read-coverage] add ddx_bead_blocked + ddx_bead_dep_tree MCP tools` | FEAT-004/002 |
| P1.4 | G4 | `[read-coverage] add MCP worker status tools (list/show/log)` | FEAT-002/013 |
| P2.1 | G5 | `[read-coverage] add agent models/catalog/capabilities HTTP + MCP` | FEAT-006 |
| P2.2 | G6 | `[read-coverage] add ddx_list_personas MCP tool` | FEAT-007/002 |
| P3.1 | G7 | `[read-coverage] add MCP process metrics tools` | FEAT-016 |
| P3.2 | G8 | `[read-coverage] add layer-aware run-history HTTP + MCP routes` | FEAT-021/010 |
| P3.3 | G9 | `[read-coverage] add MCP server registry + plugin manifest HTTP + MCP` | FEAT-009/015 |
| P4.1 | G10 | `[read-coverage] add GET /api/docs/changed REST route` | FEAT-007 |
| P4.2 | G11 | `[read-coverage] add per-metric-id history/trend HTTP + MCP` | FEAT-016 |
| P4.3 | G12 | `[read-coverage] add bead evidence + cooldown + routing HTTP + MCP` | FEAT-004 |

Commit succeeded. The audit is complete:

**What was done:**

1. **Enumerated** all CLI-visible read surfaces across bead, doc graph, agent service, execution/run, persona, process metrics, and plugin/registry namespaces.

2. **Mapped** each to HTTP REST and MCP tool coverage, finding 12 gaps (G1–G12).

3. **Created 12 gap beads** in the tracker, sequenced by user-visibility impact:
   - **P1 (blockers):** `ddx_doc_dependents` MCP tool (G1), exec run detail/log MCP (G2), bead blocked + dep-tree MCP (G3), worker status MCP (G4)
   - **P2 (configuration transparency):** agent models/catalog/capabilities HTTP+MCP (G5), personas list MCP (G6)
   - **P3 (process intelligence):** process metrics MCP (G7), layer-aware run-history routes (G8), server registry + plugin manifest (G9)
   - **P4 (completeness):** `GET /api/docs/changed` REST (G10), per-metric-id history/trend (G11), bead evidence/cooldown/routing (G12)

4. **Committed** the audit document to `docs/helix/02-design/read-coverage-audit-2026-04-29.md`.
